generations.generated_text:
NCB and DS-1000 are distinct benchmarks with different focuses. DS-1000 is specifically for data science code generation with a focus on data science libraries. NCB, on the other hand, is designed to mirror real-world coding tasks across multiple domains including software, front-end, system administration, and AI, making it more comprehensive in terms of real-world engineering issues. 

NCB introduces a semi-automated pipeline to improve efficiency in constructing test cases, which reduces the time and manpower required. This is a significant improvement over manual construction processes, which is a key point in the provided text.

Option A is incorrect because NCB does not generate a more extensive test set than DS-1000 and does not open-source 140 test cases as stated.

Option B is partially correct but not the best answer as it's too vague and doesn't capture the core technical improvement.

Option C directly addresses the key technical improvement: the semi-automated pipeline reducing data pressure (time and manpower) in benchmark construction.

Option D is false as DS-1000 is not NCB's follow-up work.

C
